skill.name: stripe-handler
skill.description: Guide custom Stripe Checkout, webhook handling, and background fulfillment for payments outside standard plans or credit packages.
skill.summary: Implement custom Stripe payment flows with metadata, webhooks, and Inngest fulfillment.
skill.author: AayushBaniya2006

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged many shell, environment, and sensitive-file patterns, but the reviewed files are Markdown guidance and examples rather than executable skill code. The command findings are Stripe CLI testing instructions, and the environment findings use app URL variables and placeholder Stripe secret names with no evidence of real secrets, exfiltration, or prompt injection. The skill is publishable with low risk because implementers must still handle payment secrets and fulfillment logic carefully.

low_findings.title: Markdown Command Examples Flagged as Shell Execution
low_findings.description: The static command detections are false positives for executable skill behavior. The files contain Markdown documentation and Stripe CLI testing examples, not code that automatically runs shell commands.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed locations are documentation lines and fenced examples. No executable script, hook, or tool wrapper invokes these commands automatically.

low_findings.title: Environment Variable Guidance Uses Secret Placeholders
low_findings.description: The environment detections are mostly expected for Stripe integration guidance. The files mention application URLs and placeholder Stripe secret variable names, but no real credential value or exfiltration path is present.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The evidence shows configuration names and placeholders used in normal Stripe setup. Risk remains low because implementers will handle real payment secrets in their own projects.

low_findings.title: Custom Payment Fulfillment Requires Implementation Review
low_findings.description: The skill advises metadata-based payment fulfillment and idempotent database updates. This is legitimate, but users must validate server-created checkout inputs and avoid trusting client-controlled product or user identifiers.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The concern is semantic rather than malicious. The documentation recommends metadata and idempotency, but final security depends on how the consuming application validates checkout creation and webhook fulfillment.

content.value_statement: Custom payment flows often need webhook logic outside standard plans or credits. This skill guides Stripe Checkout sessions, metadata routing, and Inngest fulfillment.
actual_capabilities: Identifies when a custom Stripe flow should be used instead of plan or credit handlers.
Guides server-side Stripe Checkout session creation with purchase metadata.
Shows how to route checkout completion events to custom fulfillment logic.
Recommends Inngest background processing for webhook work that may timeout.
Lists required Stripe environment variables and local webhook testing steps.
Emphasizes idempotent database updates for duplicate webhook deliveries.
limitations: Does not provide a complete production implementation or database migration.
Does not validate product pricing, user authorization, or business rules by itself.
Assumes existing Stripe, Inngest, and application route conventions.
Requires careful handling of Stripe secrets and webhook signature verification.

use_cases.title: Sell one-time digital goods
use_cases.description: Create a custom checkout flow for courses, downloads, or premium content outside subscription plans.

use_cases.title: Add service payment fulfillment
use_cases.description: Attach metadata to Stripe sessions and fulfill paid services after checkout completion.

use_cases.title: Move webhook work to background jobs
use_cases.description: Keep Stripe webhook responses fast while processing database updates and notifications in Inngest.

prompt_templates.title: Choose the payment flow
prompt_templates.prompt: Use stripe-handler to decide whether my new payment should use a custom Stripe flow, plans-handler, or credits-handler.

prompt_templates.title: Create checkout session guidance
prompt_templates.prompt: Use stripe-handler to design a server-side Stripe Checkout session for a one-time product with metadata and success pages.

prompt_templates.title: Wire custom webhook fulfillment
prompt_templates.prompt: Use stripe-handler to update my Stripe checkout completion handling for a custom product and idempotent fulfillment.

prompt_templates.title: Design background processing
prompt_templates.prompt: Use stripe-handler to move custom payment fulfillment from the Stripe webhook into an Inngest background function.

output_examples.input: I need a one-time course checkout outside my normal subscription plans.
output_examples.output: A payment flow plan with a server route, Stripe Checkout metadata, redirect behavior, and webhook fulfillment steps.

output_examples.input: My Stripe webhook may timeout when it unlocks paid content and sends email.
output_examples.output: A background processing design that acknowledges Stripe quickly and moves fulfillment work into an Inngest function.

output_examples.input: I want to test my custom checkout event locally before release.
output_examples.output: A local testing checklist covering Stripe CLI forwarding, checkout completion events, and environment variables.

best_practices: Keep checkout metadata small, specific, and useful for routing fulfillment.
Validate users, products, and prices on the server before creating checkout sessions.
Make webhook fulfillment idempotent so duplicate Stripe events do not double-grant access.
anti_patterns: Do not use this skill for standard subscription plans or standard credit packages.
Do not trust client-supplied price IDs or user IDs without server-side validation.
Do not perform slow external work inside the Stripe webhook response path.

faq.question: When should I use this skill?
faq.answer: Use it for custom Stripe payments that are not standard subscription plans or credit packages.

faq.question: Does it create Stripe products for me?
faq.answer: No. It guides application code for checkout sessions, metadata, webhooks, and fulfillment.

faq.question: Can it handle recurring payments?
faq.answer: It can guide custom recurring flows, but standard SaaS plans should use the plan handler instead.

faq.question: Why does it recommend metadata?
faq.answer: Metadata helps the webhook identify the purchase type and route fulfillment to the right logic.

faq.question: Why use Inngest with Stripe webhooks?
faq.answer: Inngest lets the webhook respond quickly while heavier fulfillment work runs in the background.

faq.question: Is this a complete security solution?
faq.answer: No. You still need server-side validation, webhook signature checks, secret management, and idempotency controls.
